description: Blazor component directly injects IPlayerRepository and calls it in OnInitialized. Architecture rules require components to call services only; repositories should be accessed exclusively by services.
suggestedFix: Remove @inject IPlayerRepository PlayerRepository from Players.razor. Create or extend IPlayerService/IPlayerDisplayService with a method like GetTeamPlayers(Guid teamId) that internally calls the repository. The component should only inject service interfaces.

description: Guid.Empty is hardcoded as the teamId argument in GetByTeamId(Guid.Empty). This couples the view to a magic sentinel value and bypasses any team-context resolution that should live in a service.
suggestedFix: Move the team ID resolution into a service method. The service should be responsible for knowing which team is active (e.g., via IGameStateService or similar), not the Blazor component.

description: GetByTeamId filters players by TeamId inside the repository (.Where(p => p.TeamId == teamId)). The repository guide classifies filtering/domain logic as business logic that should live in a service, not in a repository method.
suggestedFix: Expose a simple GetAll() method that returns all players as IReadOnlyList<Player>, then let a service layer perform the TeamId filter. Alternatively, keep GetByTeamId but document the design decision if team-scoped access is deemed a data-access concern rather than business logic.
